title: Checking accessibility with Accessibility Checker
what_it_is: Accessibility Checker is a built-in Microsoft 365 tool that scans a document, presentation or spreadsheet and flags anything that could be hard for someone using assistive technology to access.
why_use_it: It catches common accessibility problems — like missing alt text or a poor heading structure — before a resource is shared, rather than someone finding out afterwards that it doesn't work for them.
how_to: Open your file in Word, PowerPoint or Excel.
Go to the Review tab and select Check Accessibility.
Work through the Errors, Warnings and Tips listed in the pane on the right.
Select each issue to see exactly where it is and how to fix it.
Re-run the check after making changes to confirm the issues are cleared.
